parameters.content:
## Invoice Reminder & Collections Ladder

### How it works

This workflow runs daily at 8 AM via a cron trigger, fetches all overdue invoices from Clio's API, and calculates how many days each has been unpaid. Invoices that match a ladder step today (day 1, 7, 14, 30, or 60+) proceed to the reminder flow. Invoices at 60+ days go to a direct partner alert email. Earlier ladder steps build a client reminder email appropriate to the escalation level and send it through the Bar-Compliance Guardrail. Clio automatically removes paid invoices from the overdue set, so payment stops reminders with no extra logic.

### Setup steps

- Set FIRM_NAME, FIRM_EMAIL, FIRM_FROM_EMAIL, FIRM_PARTNER_EMAIL, FIRM_PAY_LINK, CLIO_BASE_URL, and GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID in n8n project Variables.
- Wire a Clio API (Header Auth) credential and an SMTP email credential in project → Credentials tab.
- Deploy the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow first and set its ID in GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID.
- Toggle the workflow Active to start the daily schedule.

### Customization

Change the cron expression in Daily Invoice Check to run at a different time or on weekdays only. Edit the ladder step day windows in Normalize & Triage Invoices if your billing cycle differs. Adjust the email body tone for each ladder step in Build Client Reminder Email.

## Fetch and triage invoices

Runs every morning, pulls all overdue invoices from Clio, calculates days overdue for each, and identifies which invoices hit a reminder ladder step today.

## No invoices today

Ends the run quietly when no invoices fall on a ladder step. Visible in execution history for audit purposes.

## Partner escalation

Invoices at 60+ days skip the client reminder and go directly to the managing partner for manual follow-up — a call, a payment plan offer, or a collections referral.

## Send client reminder

Builds the appropriate escalation email (Day 1 friendly through Day 30 firm), routes it through the Bar-Compliance Guardrail for opt-out checking and footer insertion, then sends it.

nodes.name: Daily Invoice Check
nodes.notes:
Fires every day at 8am server time. Each run checks Clio for overdue invoices and sends whichever ladder step is due today.

Running daily (not weekday-only) ensures invoices that fall due on a Friday are not missed over the weekend.

To change the run time: edit the cron expression. Format: minute hour * * * (0 8 * * * = 8am every day).

nodes.name: Fetch Overdue Invoices from Clio
nodes.notes:
Pulls all overdue invoices from Clio in a single request. Clio automatically removes paid invoices from the overdue set — so when a client pays, their invoice disappears from this query and the reminder ladder stops with no extra logic needed.

EU firms: set CLIO_BASE_URL to https://eu.app.clio.com

Returns up to 200 invoices per run. If your firm regularly has more than 200 overdue invoices at once, add a loop over the `page` parameter.

Credential: Clio API (Bearer token). Clio personal API tokens do not expire automatically, but rotate yours every 90 days as a security best practice.

nodes.name: Normalize & Triage Invoices
nodes.notes:
Computes how many days each overdue invoice has been unpaid and assigns a ladder step. Returns one item per invoice that is due for a reminder today.

Ladder windows use a 2-day range (e.g. days 1–2, 7–8) so a reminder is not missed if the cron runs a day late due to a server restart or holiday.

Invoices with a zero or negative balance are skipped — Clio should not return these on the overdue endpoint, but this guards against it.

If no invoices are due for a reminder today, returns a single sentinel item with is_actionable: false, which the next node routes to a quiet skip.

nodes.name: If Invoice Due Today
nodes.notes:
Routes invoices that hit a ladder step today to the reminder flow. Routes days with no matching invoices to a labelled Skip so you can see a clean record in your execution history.

True (right output): invoice is actionable — continue to partner check or client reminder.
False (bottom output): no reminders due today — stop.

nodes.name: Skip — no invoices due for reminder today
nodes.notes: No actionable invoices today. The workflow run is recorded in execution history for audit purposes. No message is sent.

nodes.name: If Partner Escalation
nodes.notes:
Invoices at 60+ days overdue go directly to the managing partner for manual intervention — a personal call or referral to collections.

The partner alert bypasses the client-facing compliance guardrail because it is an internal firm email, not a client communication.

True (right output): day60 — alert the partner.
False (bottom output): day1, 7, 14, or 30 — send the standard client reminder through the compliance guardrail.

nodes.name: Build Partner Alert
nodes.notes:
Builds the partner escalation email for invoices 60+ days overdue. Includes client name, matter reference, balance, and a direct pay link.

The pay link uses Clio's share_url for the invoice if available (a direct client-specific payment page), falling back to the FIRM_PAY_LINK variable.

This is an internal email — no opt-out footer or compliance guardrail needed.

nodes.name: Alert Partner — Invoice 60+ Days Overdue
nodes.notes:
Sends the 60-day escalation alert to the managing partner. The partner can then call the client directly, offer a payment plan, or refer to collections.

Set FIRM_PARTNER_EMAIL in n8n Variables (project → Variables tab) to the partner's email address.

Credential: Email account (SMTP) — same SMTP account used for client reminders.

nodes.name: Build Client Reminder Email
nodes.notes:
Builds the client-facing reminder email for the appropriate ladder step. Tone escalates from friendly (Day 1) to firm (Day 30).

The pay link uses Clio's per-invoice share_url when available — this gives clients a direct, pre-populated payment page. Falls back to FIRM_PAY_LINK if Clio does not return a share_url.

Do not add an opt-out footer here — the compliance guardrail (next node) appends it automatically. The guardrail also logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

Variables required: FIRM_NAME, FIRM_EMAIL, FIRM_PAY_LINK.

nodes.name: Compliance Check — Invoice Reminder
nodes.notes:
Checks whether this client has opted out of billing email reminders. If not, appends the required unsubscribe footer and logs the send attempt to your audit sheet.

The guardrail workflow ID is read from the GUARDRAIL_WORKFLOW_ID n8n Variable (project → Variables tab). Set it to the numeric ID from the Bar-Compliance Guardrail workflow URL: .../workflow/WORKFLOW_ID.

Prerequisite: Bar-Compliance Guardrail must be deployed and active.

Returns: { approved, message_out, recipient, suppression_reason } plus all upstream fields.

nodes.name: If Approved to Send Reminder
nodes.notes:
Checks the guardrail's decision before sending.

True (right output): client is reachable and has not opted out — send the reminder.
False (bottom output): client has opted out — route to Skip and do not send.

nodes.name: Send Invoice Reminder
nodes.notes:
Sends the compliance-approved invoice reminder to the client. Uses message_out from the guardrail, which includes the required unsubscribe footer.

The subject line is pulled from the Build Client Reminder Email node (the guardrail does not modify subjects).

Credential: Email account (SMTP). Set FIRM_FROM_EMAIL in n8n Variables to the sender address clients will see.

nodes.name: Skip — client opted out
nodes.notes: This client has opted out of billing reminders. The suppression is logged by the guardrail workflow. No email is sent.

main.node: Skip — no invoices due for reminder today
